title: Blox AI model manifest (/etc/fula/ai-manifest.json)
description: Phase 18 contract. CDN-published manifest that controls which model version a device runs. download_model.sh fetches this on each plugin (re)start and selects either `current` or `rollback` based on `rollback_required`. Lets the developer ship a one-line manifest update to flip a fleet back to a known-good model version without re-pushing the plugin or coordinating with each device. Both `current` and `rollback` are required so a rollback is always possible — refusing to ship a manifest with `rollback: null` is a deliberate operational discipline (if you can't roll back, you shouldn't roll forward).

schema_version.description: Bumped on breaking changes to manifest shape. Plugin refuses to parse manifests with unknown schema_version.

current.description: The model version the fleet SHOULD be running when rollback_required=false.

rollback.description: The known-good prior version. Selected on every plugin restart when rollback_required=true. Operationally, this is the model the developer would point to if `current` turns out to misbehave in production. SHOULD be different from `current` (a manifest with current==rollback is technically valid but operationally pointless — log a warning at load time).

rollback_required.description: When true, the next plugin restart picks `rollback`. The plugin does NOT auto-restart on manifest change — operator (or a future OTA hook) triggers the restart. Once published as true, devices migrate to rollback over the natural cadence of plugin restarts.

manifest_version.description: Optional monotone counter for cache-busting + drift detection. Not enforced by the schema's required list (forward compat with CDN tooling that adds it later).

published_at.description: Optional. When the manifest was published. Useful for forensic 'what was the active version at time T?' queries.

model_version.description: Opaque version string. Convention: ISO-like date or semver. Used in the local cache filename so different versions can coexist on disk during a swap.

url.description: HTTPS-only. The CDN MUST serve the model file at this URL with a stable byte-for-byte response across restarts (so the SHA below stays valid).

sha256.description: Lowercase hex SHA-256 of the model file at `url`. Plugin refuses to swap to a downloaded file whose SHA doesn't match.

size_bytes.description: Expected file size in bytes (≥1 GB, ≤20 GB). Used as an early-fail check before the slow SHA computation on slow links.

rkllm_version.description: Optional. RKLLM toolkit version that produced this .rkllm file. Plugin can warn (not refuse) when the container's expected RKLLM version doesn't match.
